merger_name: Energy Bay - Rights to install/operate embedded networks and other assets from Centuria Capital Group members

acquirers.name: The Trustee for Energy Bay Kappa Fund

anzsic_codes.name: On Selling Electricity and Electricity Market Operation

merger_description:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa Fund (**Energy Bay**) proposes to acquire from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the **CCG Members**):

* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,
* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and
* first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in respect of any further sites of the kind described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application.

Energy Bay is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South Wales (**NSW**), Australian Capital Territory (**ACT**), Western Australia (**WA**), Victoria (**VIC**) and Queensland (**QLD**). Energy Bay Group also owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd (**Diamond Energy**), an electricity retailer that operates in all National Electricity Market (**NEM**) jurisdictions.

The CCG Members are subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group, which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (**CNI**) and Centuria Funds Management Limited (**CFML**). CNI is an ASX-listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria Capital Fund. The CCG Members invest in and manage properties for investment funds. As part of that primary business activity, in each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia (**SA**), and WA, they also:

* operate embedded network infrastructure at some of their sites,
* engage in behind the meter generation or storage at their sites, and
* supply and sell electricity to their commercial tenants connected to their embedded networks (**embedded network services**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa
Fund (Energy Bay), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of:
a. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to
operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,
b. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install
and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and
c. first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in
respect of any further sites of the kind described in the
transaction documents,
from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the CCG
Members), which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (CNI) and
Centuria Funds Management Limited (CFML), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.

The acquirer, Energy Bay, is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a
distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded
networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South
Wales (NSW), Australian Capital Territory (ACT), Western Australia
(WA), Victoria (VIC) and Queensland (QLD). Energy Bay Group also
owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd, an electricity retailer that operates in
all National Electricity Market (NEM) jurisdictions.
The vendors, the CCG Members, are subsidiaries of the Centuria
Capital Group, which is comprised of CNI and CFML. CNI is an ASX-
listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and
unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third
party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria
Capital Fund.
In each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia, and WA, the CCG
Members:
a. operate embedded network infrastructure,
b. engage in behind the meter generation and storage at their
embedded network sites, and
c. supply and sell electricity to commercial tenants connected
to their embedded networks.
(together, Embedded Network Services). Through the Acquisition,
Energy Bay Group will increase its share of supply of Embedded
Network Services in WA and the NEM.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:
a. there is meaningful horizontal overlap between Energy Bay
Group and the CCG Members in the supply of Embedded
Network Services in WA and the NEM, and Energy Bay
appears to be one of the larger suppliers of Embedded
Network Services in the NEM
b. the extent to which Energy Bay Group is likely to be
constrained by alternative suppliers of Embedded Network
Services post-acquisition is uncertain
c. there is a vertical relationship between the Embedded
Network Services supplied by the parties and the electricity
retailing operations of Energy Bay Group’s subsidiary,
Diamond Energy, and the impact of the Acquisition on
Energy Bay Group’s ability and/or incentive to foreclose
rivals warrants further consideration.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a
concluded view on the likelihood of the Acquisition meeting the
notification thresholds. However, the ACCC notes that based on the
Australian revenue and transaction value figures provided, the
Acquisition may meet the monetary notification thresholds in ss 2-1
and 2-3 of the Competition and Consumer (Notification of
Acquisitions) Determination 2025.
For these reasons, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
